B.x: A comprehensive showcase of all 20 Vela block types with realistic, production-quality content. Dark theme — #0F172A background, #E2E8F0 text, #3B82F6 accent.

The heading block defines visual hierarchy and draws the eye. The text block delivers body copy at configurable sizes and weights. Together they form the backbone of every slide — clear, scannable, and brand-consistent across any theme.
Design is not just what it looks like and feels like. Design is how it works — and great tools disappear into the work.
B.author: Steve Jobs

B.x: Use callouts sparingly — one per slide maximum. They work best for warnings, tips, and key takeaways that must not be missed by the audience.
B.title: Design Principle

I: Badges convey status at a glance using color and concise labels
Callouts interrupt visual flow intentionally — use for critical alerts
Bullets organize parallel ideas without adding visual noise
Maintain consistent bullet depth: two levels maximum for clarity

S.n: Steps Guide Users Through Any Process

B.x: Steps Guide Users Through Any Process

I.title: Install the SDK
I.x: Run npm install @vela/sdk or pip install vela-sdk. Requires Node 18+ or Python 3.10+.

I.title: Authenticate
I.x: Set VELA_API_KEY in your environment. The client auto-reads it — no hardcoded secrets.

I.title: Author Your Deck
I.x: Write a compact DSL JSON file. Define sections, slides, blocks, and a color palette.

I.title: Ship to Production
I.x: Run vela deck ship deck.json --output slides.jsx. Deploy the JSX to any React host.

B.x: Images & Code Make Abstract Ideas Concrete

S.n: SVG, Spacers & Dividers Complete the Toolkit

B.x: SVG, Spacers & Dividers Complete the Toolkit

<svg viewBox="0 0 520 110" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><defs><linearGradient id="gr1" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="0%"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#3B82F6"/><stop offset="50%" stop-color="#10B981"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#F59E0B"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="520" height="110" rx="12" fill="#1E293B"/><circle cx="55" cy="55" r="38" fill="url(#gr1)" opacity="0.9"/><text x="55" y="62" text-anchor="middle" fill="white" font-size="26" font-weight="800" font-family="sans-serif">V</text><rect x="115" y="24" width="380" height="14" rx="7" fill="url(#gr1)" opacity="0.8"/><rect x="115" y="48" width="300" height="14" rx="7" fill="url(#gr1)" opacity="0.55"/><rect x="115" y="72" width="220" height="14" rx="7" fill="url(#gr1)" opacity="0.3"/></svg>

Spacers are bare integers (e.g. 8, 16, 24) that inject precise vertical breathing room between blocks. Dividers draw a horizontal rule — optionally with a centered label — to section off content within a slide.

S.n: 20 Block Types. One Unified Design System.

B.x: 20 Block Types. One Unified Design System.
Every block type demonstrated with realistic content on a dark #0F172A background, #E2E8F0 text, and #3B82F6 accent. Each slide carries a badge identifying the blocks it showcases.
